title: centaurea kerneriana subsp. gheorghieffii
intro: Centaurea kerneriana subsp. gheorghieffii, also known as the Kerner's knapweed or mountain centaury, is a perennial plant belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae), native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, derived from its bioactive compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Centaurea kerneriana subsp. gheorghieffii include the potential treatment of various conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin disorders, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. The plant can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine as a natural remedy for its reported health benefits.
